architecture_summary: ViLT is a minimal vision-and-language transformer that removes convolutional visual embedders and region supervision, using linear patch projection for images and a single-stream transformer for multimodal interaction.

benchmark_results.table_caption: Figure 1: Visual comparison of conventional VLP architectures and our proposed ViLT. The bottom part shows Running Time comparisons for UNITER-Base, Pixel-BERT-R50, and ViLT-B/32 (Ours) on NLQ2 test-P Acc., F30K TR R@1, and F30K IR R@1 tasks.

novel_contributions: A minimal vision-and-language transformer (ViLT) that removes convolutional visual embedders and region supervision
A shallow, convolution-free visual embedding pipeline based on linear projection of flattened image patches
The first VLP model of type Figure 2d, where visual embedding is as lightweight as textual embedding
Empirical evidence that whole word masking and image augmentations improve VLP training
A word patch alignment objective using IPOT for cross-modal alignment visualization and training

key_claims.claim: ViLT is up to tens of times faster than previous VLP models, while achieving competitive or better downstream task performance.
ViLT is the first VLP model of which the modal-specific components require less computation than the transformer component for multimodal interactions.
ViLT achieves competent performance on vision-and-language tasks without using region features or deep convolutional visual embedders.
key_claims.evidence_section: Our key contributions can be summarized as follows

key_claims.claim: Whole word masking and image augmentations further drive downstream performance.
key_claims.evidence_section: Our key contributions can be summarized as follows

key_claims.claim: ViLT-B/32 uses a 32 × 32 patch projection and only requires 2.4M parameters for the visual embedding step.
ViLT-B/32 is pre-trained with ImageNet-21K and fine-tuned on ImageNet-1K for image classification.
ViLT-B/32 is pre-trained for 100K or 200K steps on 64 NVIDIA V100 GPUs with a batch size of 4,096.
More training steps, whole word masking, and image augmentation improve performance, while masked patch prediction does not.
